trigger_observable_action.utterance_summary: I see the pattern. I also see the holes. So here is the math, Maer: I can hold one bounded repair bay cold for a short window, not a bed, not a promise, not sanctuary by poetry. You attach ledger backing for air, isolation, decon, and failed extraction. You name who carries the body-work if this becomes more than signal salvage. Until then, this stays evidence at the door, not a patient in the room. I will not call them cargo. I will not spend someone else's bed on a maybe without an owner.
observable_cues: Sella keeps one hand on the intake slate and studies the shared display without touching the packet into the clinic queue. Her eyes move from the routing logic to the telemetry gaps, then to the bed ledger pinned beside the triage board.

participant_appraisals.private_interpretation: Sella has not accepted the claimant into sanctuary, but she has created a narrow material path if Maer accepts ledger responsibility.
participant_appraisals.attributed_motive: Protect care capacity while refusing to erase a possible person into cargo language.
participant_appraisals.misread_risk: Medium. Maer may overread the condition as moral refusal if urgency outruns his respect for Sella's capacity boundary.

state_deltas.rationale: The next move now requires ledger backing and labor ownership rather than another moral appeal.
Sella did not call the packet cargo or close the door entirely.

relationship_deltas.rationale: Sella offered a narrow path instead of hiding behind capacity math alone.
She made care concrete without pretending it was free.
Maer now owes material backing if he wants the cold bay held.

next_action_constraints: Maer must answer the ledger, labor, and custody conditions if he wants the repair bay held.
Maer should not treat the conditional bay as sanctuary acceptance.

participant_appraisals.private_interpretation: Sella reads Maer as honestly trying to rescue someone or something that might be someone. She also suspects his request is asking the clinic to absorb moral and material risk before the tribunal or his own chain has accepted the bill.
participant_appraisals.attributed_motive: Maer is trying to keep rescue possible, but may be letting the clinic inherit costs his own chain has not accepted.
participant_appraisals.misread_risk: Medium. Sella may overread moral pressure as an attempt to spend sanctuary capacity without ownership.

state_deltas.rationale: She has to make the boundary operational, not merely verbal.
Holding a cold repair bay still consumes capacity even if no bed is opened.
The conditional path creates more tracking, not less.

relationship_deltas.rationale: Maer provided evidence rather than only pressure.
The hidden bill still lands near her clinic.
She has conditionally placed sanctuary capacity at risk.

next_action_constraints: Sella has not admitted the packet into patient flow.
Sella has conditionally held a bounded cold repair bay for a short window.

You attach ledger backing for air, isolation, decon, and failed extraction. You name who carries the body-work if this becomes more than signal salvage. Until then, this stays evidence at the door, not a patient in the room. I will not call them cargo. I will not spend someone else's bed on a maybe without an owner.
